sprint: Guardrails — Artwork Places Become Moveable + Fully Reorderable
task: Closes a real Builder-to-Creator wiring gap: World Builder's own per-Place permission block (Can a Story Author move this? / ...change this? / ...see this at all?) wrote correctly to holder.permissions, but convergeScene (tools/world-builder-v2/js/services/builder.js) never copied it into the compiled placeRects entries at all -- the guardrails had zero effect in Creator. Fixed at the source: placeRects now carries visible/moveable/editable per Place, mirroring convergeSceneLayer's own proven derivation (absent -> true at compile time, matching what Builder itself stamps on a live Holder). Creator's own render-time default is deliberately the OPPOSITE for an ALREADY-compiled Layout (absent -> locked/non-moveable, always-visible), so every theme published before this fix renders unchanged -- SlideRenderer.getPlacePermissions() is the one shared resolver both sides read. Per the user's own explicit scope choices: a small grab-handle appears on a selected, moveable Place (dragging it repositions the Place via the same generic elementOverrides bag every other draggable object already uses, completely separate from the existing content-pan gesture); and Places now join the SAME fully-interleaved, freely-reorderable pass Stickers and World-owned slide/overlay Layer Pack objects already used (previously only for Quote/zero-Holder pages, now every Story-role page) -- full cross-object reorder, not just among Places. Object Strip gained real id/draggable wiring for Place cards (reusing the existing lock-badge/drag-to-reorder machinery verbatim) and a duplicate-card bug (Places were being rendered twice, once by their own loop and once by Object Strip's generic render-tree loop) was found and fixed in the same pass. A real, separate pre-existing bug was found and fixed while verifying the grab-handle on a landscape Scene: js/app.js's _canvasCoords called SlideRenderer.getCanvasSize() with no slide argument, always resolving the portrait default regardless of the page's real aspect, so every mouse hit-test on a non-portrait page was silently computed against the wrong logical size -- fixed by passing the current slide through. A second real regression was caught by full regression testing before shipping: the first cut of the Place-inclusion logic required a real placeRects array to include any Place at all, silently dropping Place 1 (and Museum Gallery's own Frame-scoped Wax Seal/Museum Caption Layer Pack content) for every theme compiled before this feature -- fixed by treating an absent placeRects array as exactly one implicit Place, matching the same convention getPlaceRects' own fallback already establishes elsewhere in this file. Verified via a new Playwright suite (permission resolution, Object Strip lock-badge/draggable state, cross-object reorder between a Place and a Sticker, grab-handle hitbox gating, a real mouse-driven drag repositioning a Place, and a visible:false Place skipped entirely) plus full regression across the existing 31-script Creator/Builder Playwright suite (one script's own assertion was intentionally updated for the new, wider reorder scope -- a Sticker and a World-owned Shape on any Story-role page now correctly share one bucket, not a regression) and both goldenBuild.js suites, all passing, zero console errors.
